verdicts.rationale: NPPES taxonomy lists Internal Medicine, Medical Oncology, meeting the specialty requirement.
verdicts.requirement: The investigator must be a medical oncologist or breast cancer specialist capable of managing adjuvant endocrine therapy decisions in ER+/HER2- early breast cancer.
verdicts.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy code lists the physician under Medical Oncology, Hematology & Oncology, or a breast oncology subspecialty.

verdicts.rationale: Multiple PubMed publications directly address endocrine therapy and CDK4/6 inhibitors in breast cancer, including palbociclib with adjuvant/endocrine therapy trials and camizestrant PRO data.
verdicts.requirement: Demonstrated familiarity with endocrine therapy and CDK4/6 inhibitor use in early breast cancer, evidenced by scholarly contribution.
verdicts.satisfies_if: At least one PubMed-indexed publication authored by the physician on endocrine therapy, SERDs/SERMs, aromatase inhibitors, or CDK4/6 inhibitors in breast cancer.

verdicts.rationale: Medicare claims show an active oncology outpatient/hospital practice (206 services, 134 beneficiaries) consistent with a breast oncology caseload, but this is a floor only and does not specifically confirm volume of resected ER+/HER2- intermediate-high/high risk patients.
verdicts.requirement: The practice must see a meaningful volume of post-surgical ER+/HER2- early breast cancer patients at intermediate-high or high recurrence risk to plausibly identify and enroll eligible patients.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician self-reports an active caseload of resected ER+/HER2- early breast cancer patients meeting intermediate-high/high risk criteria, or Medicare claims data shows a floor volume of breast cancer diagnosis/treatment visits consistent with an active breast oncology practice.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report or other evidence in the dossier addresses tumor board participation or referral pathways with surgical/radiation oncology.
verdicts.requirement: Site needs an established referral relationship with breast surgeons and radiation oncologists to capture patients promptly after definitive locoregional therapy, before recurrence risk staging becomes stale.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician or site self-reports participation in a multidisciplinary breast cancer program or tumor board with surgical/radiation oncology referral pathways.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report evidence on long-term follow-up coordination or patient tracking systems is present in the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: Site must be able to retain and follow patients through a 7-year treatment period and up to 14 years of follow-up for the primary endpoint.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site self-reports established long-duration trial retention processes (e.g., dedicated long-term follow-up coordination, patient tracking systems) suitable for multi-year adjuvant trials.

verdicts.rationale: No self-report evidence regarding pharmacy or drug accountability infrastructure is present in the dossier.
verdicts.requirement: Site must be equipped to dispense, track, and monitor adherence for multiple oral agents across randomized arms.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Site self-reports pharmacy or drug accountability infrastructure for oral investigational and comparator agents.

verdicts.rationale: Physician is listed as an overall official on multiple randomized multi-arm trials such as PALLAS (NCT02040857) and TRADE (NCT06001762), demonstrating experience managing multi-arm randomized designs relevant to this trial's structure.
verdicts.requirement: Site must be able to operationally manage an open-label, multi-arm randomized design spanning five comparator regimens plus an optional abemaciclib add-on.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician or site self-reports prior experience running open-label randomized trials with multiple comparator arms, or is listed as investigator on a comparable multi-arm study in clinicaltrials.gov.

verdicts.rationale: Physician is a confirmed overall official on Phase III breast cancer trials including PALLAS (NCT02040857) and PALbociclib CoLlaborative Adjuvant Study (NCT02513394), both adjuvant endocrine therapy/CDK4/6i trials in early breast cancer.
verdicts.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience as site investigator on a Phase III (or other late-phase) breast cancer trial, given the scale and registrational nature of this study.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as PI or sub-investigator on at least one completed or ongoing Phase 2/3 breast cancer trial in clinicaltrials.gov.

verdicts.rationale: Open Payments records show multiple research-category payments from Roche and Bristol-Myers Squibb tied to named oncology studies, indicating a track record of industry-sponsored research.
verdicts.requirement: A track record of receiving industry clinical research payments suggests established capacity to conduct sponsor-funded oncology trials.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research-category payments to the physician from a pharmaceutical sponsor in oncology.

verdicts.rationale: Physician is listed as overall official on several completed industry-relevant oncology trials (e.g., NCT02040857 PALLAS, NCT01827787 Eribulin, NCT01982448), demonstrating prior conduct on completed multi-site oncology trials.
verdicts.requirement: Demonstrated GCP-compliant conduct on prior large, industry-sponsored, multi-site oncology trials similar in scale to this 798-site study.
verdicts.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as an investigator on at least one completed industry-sponsored oncology trial registered on clinicaltrials.gov.

narrative: Erica Mayer presents strong expertise and trial-execution signals: confirmed medical oncology specialty, a substantial endocrine therapy/CDK4/6i publication record, and confirmed overall-official roles on multiple Phase III and multi-arm adjuvant breast cancer trials (PALLAS, PALCAS, TRADE), plus documented industry research funding. These make her a credible investigator for a long-horizon, multi-arm adjuvant endocrine trial like CAMBRIA-2. The weakest area is operational/site-level readiness — there is no self-reported evidence on long-term retention infrastructure, oral agent dispensing capacity, or multidisciplinary referral pathways, all of which are self-report-dependent criteria absent from this dossier. Medicare volume data supports an active oncology practice but cannot confirm the specific resected ER+/HER2- intermediate-high/high-risk population targeted by this trial. Direct site-level attestations on retention, pharmacy, and tumor board integration would substantially clarify the operational fit picture.
